_README: JSON Schema Reference for All 37 Slide Types (34 display + 3 interactive action slides). Each slide in a campaign JSON has: slide_num, component, title, content, narration, duration_seconds, audio_file. The 'component' value must exactly match one of the keys below. The 'content' object must follow the schema shown for that component. Fields marked (required) will show empty/fallback if missing. Fields marked (optional) enhance the slide when present.

slide_wrapper_example._note: This is the wrapper structure for every slide in a campaign JSON. The 'content' object varies by component type.
slide_wrapper_example.title: Short internal title for this slide
slide_wrapper_example.narration: What Sarah says aloud when this slide is displayed. Write conversational, warm, 2-4 sentences.

HeroSlide._description: Full-screen cinematic opening slide with gradient or background image. Use as slide 1 of every campaign.
HeroSlide._background: Gradient by default; uses backgroundImage URL if provided and starts with http.

content.headline: (required) Big bold headline, 3-8 words
content.backgroundImage: (optional) Full URL to background image (must start with http)
content.overlay: (optional) true to darken background image for text readability

example.headline: Your Business Deserves More Customers
example.subheadline: We help local businesses get found, get chosen, and grow revenue.

PersonalizedHeroSlide._description: Hero slide with 'Personalized For You' sparkle badge. Use when the content references the recipient's business.
PersonalizedHeroSlide._background: Gradient by default; uses visual URL as background if it starts with http.

content.headline: (required) Personalized headline, can include business name
content.visual: (optional) URL to background image (must start with http) OR descriptive text (ignored as background)
content.personalization: (optional) Array of field names used for personalization, e.g. ['business_name', 'location']

example.headline: We Built This For Your Business
example.subhead: Your listing is live and ready to claim.

ComparisonHeroSlide._description: Dark gradient hero with a side-by-side comparison (before/after, standard/featured). Great for impact slides.

comparison._note: (required) Object with comparison data. Accepts multiple shapes:

example.headline: The Featured Listing Advantage
example.subhead: See what happens when you stand out

comparison.before: 3 views per month, buried on page 4
comparison.after: 47 views per month, top of search results

ProblemSlide._description: Dark gradient slide highlighting a pain point. Use to create urgency before presenting the solution.

content.title: (required) Problem headline
content.problem: (required) 1-3 sentence description of the pain point
content.painPoints: (optional) Array of specific pain point strings

example.title: The Invisible Business Problem
example.problem: 78% of consumers search online before visiting a local business. If your business doesn't show up, you're losing customers to competitors who do.
painPoints: Customers can't find your hours or location
Outdated info drives people to competitors
No reviews means no trust with new customers
You're paying for ads but your listing is broken

SolutionSlide._description: Dark gradient slide presenting the answer to the problem. Pair with ProblemSlide.

example.title: Your Complete Online Presence
example.solution: Fibonacco creates and manages your professional business listing across every platform that matters, so customers always find accurate, compelling information about your business.
benefits: Synced across Google, Apple Maps, and 40+ directories
Automated review collection from happy customers
Monthly performance reports you can actually understand

ConceptSlide._description: Versatile teaching slide. Auto-selects dark or light background based on content type. Supports stats, bullet points, comparisons, key points, element grids, and three-pillar layouts.
ConceptSlide._theme_logic: Uses dark gradient if only points/comparison/key_point. Uses light gradient if stats/elements/three_pillars are present.

content.headline: (required) Section headline
content.stats: (optional) Array of { value, label } for stat cards
content.key_point: (optional) Highlighted takeaway string shown in accent box
content.elements: (optional) Array of { icon, label, desc } cards. Icon values: search, robot, megaphone, chart, check, star, shield, zap, globe, users, mail, phone, file, location, clock, eye, heart, target, award, newspaper, building, share, message, wifi, lock, dollar, trending, lightbulb, badge
content.three_pillars: (optional) Array of { title, desc } for 3-column layout

elements.label: SEO Optimization
elements.desc: Rank higher in local search results

elements.label: Review Management
elements.desc: Collect and respond to customer reviews

elements.label: Analytics Dashboard
elements.desc: See who's finding you and how

example_with_points.headline: Why Local Businesses Fail Online
points: No strategy for collecting customer reviews
Zero visibility into how customers find them
Spending money on ads without fixing the basics
example_with_points.key_point: Fix your foundation first. Everything else gets easier.

example_with_three_pillars.headline: The Three Pillars of Local Success

three_pillars.title: Be Found
three_pillars.desc: Accurate listings across all platforms so customers can find you

three_pillars.title: Be Chosen
three_pillars.desc: Reviews, photos, and compelling descriptions that convert searchers to customers

three_pillars.title: Be Measured
three_pillars.desc: Analytics that show exactly what's working and what needs attention

StepSlide._description: Multi-purpose instructional slide. Supports numbered steps, key fields, content types, metrics, task lists, options, and recommendations. Very flexible — use the sub-sections you need.

content.headline: (required) Section headline
content.key_fields: (optional) Array of { field, tip } for form field explanations
content.content_types: (optional) Array of { type, use_for, time? } for content format cards
content.key_metrics: (optional) Array of { metric, what_it_means } OR { metric, meaning }
content.steps: (optional) Array of { num, action, time? } for numbered process steps
content.management_tasks: (optional) Array of task strings with checkmarks
content.options: (optional) Array of option strings with bullets
content.recommendation: (optional) Highlighted recommendation string in accent banner

key_fields.tip: Write 2-3 sentences that include your top services and location
Keep these accurate — wrong hours is the #1 reason for bad reviews
Add at least 5 recent photos — listings with photos get 42% more clicks
Choose your primary category carefully — it affects where you appear in search

example_with_metrics.headline: Metrics That Matter

key_metrics.what_it_means: How many people saw your listing in search results
Percentage of viewers who clicked to learn more
People who asked for directions to your business

example_with_metrics.recommendation: Check these metrics weekly — small improvements compound over time.

StatsSlide._description: Grid of big-number stat cards. Great for impact data and metrics.

content.title: (optional, also accepts 'headline') Section heading. Defaults to 'Key Stats' if neither provided.

DataSlide._description: Similar to StatsSlide but accepts stats as either an array or an object. Flexible data display.

content.title: (optional, also accepts 'headline') Section heading
content.stats: (required) Array of { value, label, trend? } OR object { key: value } (keys become labels)

BenefitsSlide._description: Card grid showing benefits with icons. Normalizes from multiple JSON shapes.

content.headline: (required) Section headline
content.benefits: (required) Array of benefit objects. Accepts multiple shapes:

BenefitsSlide._available_icons: search, robot, megaphone, chart, check, star (defaults to check if icon missing)

benefits.title: Found Everywhere
benefits.desc: Your listing synced across 40+ directories automatically

benefits.title: More Reviews
benefits.desc: Automated review requests after every customer visit

benefits.title: Clear Analytics
benefits.desc: Monthly reports showing views, clicks, and calls

benefits.title: Always Accurate
benefits.desc: We monitor and fix incorrect info across the web

ComparisonSlide._description: Side-by-side two-column comparison with checkmark/X feature lists. Good for before/after or us-vs-them.

content.title: (optional, also accepts 'headline') Section heading. Defaults to 'Comparison'

ProcessSlide._description: Horizontal numbered step flow with arrows between steps. Good for simple 3-5 step processes.

content.title: (optional, also accepts 'headline') Section heading
content.steps: (required) Array of step objects. Accepts multiple shapes:

steps.title: Claim
steps.description: Find and claim your business listing

FlowSlide._description: Similar to ProcessSlide but with card-style steps. Good for workflow visualization.

steps.description: Customer searches for your type of business

TestimonialSlide._description: Dark gradient quote slide with author attribution. Supports nested or flat testimonial data.

content.title: (optional, also accepts 'headline') Section heading above the quote

testimonial.author: (required) Person's name
testimonial.company: (optional) Company name — combined as 'role at company'
testimonial.business_type: (optional) Type of business if no role/company

content._flat_alternative: You can also put quote, author, business_type, business, result directly on content (not nested under testimonial)
content.author: (alternative) Flat author field

testimonial.quote: Within two weeks of claiming our listing, we got 12 new phone calls. I couldn't believe how many people were searching for us and couldn't find us before.
testimonial.author: Maria Rodriguez

ProofSlide._description: Case study and/or testimonial display. Shows before/after results with optional quote. Light background.

testimonial._note: (optional) Testimonial (displayed if no case_study or in addition)
testimonial.author: (required) Author name

case_study.before: 3 Google reviews, not appearing in local search, relying 100% on foot traffic
case_study.after: 47 Google reviews (4.8 stars), #2 in local search for 'deli near me', 40% of new customers from online
case_study.results: Revenue up 35% with zero additional ad spend
case_study.quote: I didn't realize how many people were searching for exactly what we offer. We were invisible.

SocialProofSlide._description: Stats grid + testimonial combo. Light background. Good for credibility slides.

content.headline: (required) Section heading
content.stats: (optional) Array of { value, label } for stat cards

testimonial.quote: Best investment I've made for my business this year.
testimonial.author: James Chen

ResultsSlide._description: Big metric cards in a 3-column grid. Use for outcomes and achievement data.

PricingSlide._description: Pricing plan cards with features and CTAs. Supports popular plan highlighting.

content.title: (optional, also accepts 'headline') Section heading. Defaults to 'Pricing'
content.plans: (required, also accepts 'options') Array of plan objects

example.title: Simple, Transparent Pricing
example.includes: All plans include free onboarding and listing setup.

TipsSlide._description: Numbered tip cards with lightbulb icons. Good for advice and best-practice slides.

example.headline: 5 Tips to Maximize Your Listing
tips: Add at least 10 high-quality photos — listings with photos get 42% more direction requests
Respond to every review within 24 hours, even negative ones — it shows you care
Update your hours for every holiday — wrong hours are the #1 complaint
Write a description that includes your location and top 3 services
Add your menu, service list, or product catalog — it dramatically improves conversion

OverviewSlide._description: Section overview with numbered area cards or topic checklist. Light background.

content.headline: (required) Section heading
content.areas: (optional) Array of { num, name, desc } for numbered overview cards
content.topics: (optional) Array of topic strings for checklist display

areas.name: Your Current Visibility
areas.desc: Where you stand right now in local search

areas.name: The Opportunity
areas.desc: How many customers are searching for your services

areas.name: Your Action Plan
areas.desc: Exactly what to do to start getting more customers

example_with_topics.headline: Today's Agenda
topics: Why most businesses are invisible online
The 3 things that determine your ranking

TutorialIntroSlide._description: Dark gradient intro slide for tutorials/how-tos. Shows time, difficulty, and outcome expectations.

expectations.outcome: (optional) What they'll learn, e.g. 'Claim and optimize your listing'

example.headline: How to Claim Your Business Listing
example.subhead: A step-by-step guide to getting your business found online

ActionSlide._description: Action-oriented slide with play icon, instruction text, and numbered steps. Good for 'do this now' moments.

example.headline: Let's Claim Your Listing Now
example.action: Follow these steps to get your business listed in under 5 minutes.
steps: Verify your ownership via phone or email

CategorySlide._description: Grid of category tags/labels. Light background with folder icon. Good for showing business types or service categories.

IdeaSlide._description: Lightbulb-themed idea list. Light background. Good for brainstorming, suggestions, and creative content.

example.headline: Content Ideas for Your Listing
ideas: Behind-the-scenes photos of your team at work
Before-and-after photos of your best projects
Short video tour of your location

ExampleSlide._description: Single example display in a white card. Light background. Good for showing sample content, templates, or text examples.

example.headline: Example Business Description
example.example:
Downtown Deli & Bakery has been serving fresh, made-from-scratch sandwiches and pastries in the heart of Springfield since 1985.

We specialize in artisan breads, custom cakes, and catering for events of all sizes. Stop by for lunch or call ahead for your next celebration.

Open Monday-Saturday, 7am-6pm. Free parking available behind the building.

StorySlide._description: Narrative text display with book icon. Light background. Good for case narratives, origin stories, and longer-form content.

example.headline: How Maria Found Her Customers
example.story:
Maria Rodriguez opened her restaurant three years ago in a busy part of town. Despite great food and loyal regulars, new customers were rare.

She didn't realize that when people searched 'Mexican restaurant near me,' her business didn't show up at all. Her competitor two blocks away had 200 reviews and a complete listing.

After claiming and optimizing her Fibonacco listing, Maria went from 3 Google reviews to 47 in just 90 days. New customers started walking in saying 'I found you on Google.'

Her revenue is up 35% — without spending a dollar on advertising.

ApplicationSlide._description: Form field preview with file icon. Light background. Shows what fields are needed for an application or signup.

IntegrationSlide._description: Two-column grid of integration/platform cards with link icon. Light background.

integrations.name: Google Business Profile
integrations.description: Your listing on Google Search and Maps

integrations.name: Bing Places
integrations.description: Don't miss the 10% who use Bing

DistributionSlide._description: Two-column grid of distribution channel cards with share icon. Light background.

ListingPreviewSlide._description: Business listing card preview. Light background. Shows a mockup of what their listing will look like.

listing_preview.placeholder_image: (optional) true to show placeholder building icon image
listing_preview.status: (optional) Status badge text, e.g. 'Ready to Claim'

example.headline: Your Listing Preview

listing_preview.description: Fresh sandwiches, artisan bread, and custom cakes made daily since 1985. Catering available for events of all sizes.

example.note: This is a preview. After claiming, you can customize every detail.

CouponPreviewSlide._description: Coupon/offer card with dashed border. Light background. Shows a deal or promotional offer.

coupon_preview.offer: (required) Offer headline, e.g. '20% Off Your First Visit'
coupon_preview.redemption: (optional) How to redeem, e.g. 'Show this screen at checkout'

example.headline: Exclusive Offer for New Listings

coupon_preview.terms: Available for new business listings only. One per business.
coupon_preview.redemption: Automatically applied when you claim your listing today

example.note: No credit card required to get started.

EventPreviewSlide._description: Event card with calendar/clock/location details. Light background. For upcoming events, webinars, or workshops.

content.headline: (required) Section heading
content.note: (optional) Note text below the event card

example.headline: Join Our Next Workshop

event_preview.title: Local SEO Masterclass for Small Business Owners
event_preview.description: Learn the exact steps to make your business the #1 result in local search. Live Q&A included.

example.note: Free for all Fibonacco members. Recording available afterward.

PreviewSlide._description: Generic key-value preview card. Light background with eye icon. Renders any object as formatted rows. Good for showing settings, configurations, or feature summaries.

content.headline: (optional, also accepts 'title') Section heading. Defaults to 'Preview'
content.preview: (optional) Object with key-value pairs to display. Booleans show as check/uncheck. Nested objects render as sub-sections.
content.features: (optional) Array of feature strings shown as checklist below the preview card

example.headline: Your Listing Settings
example.note: You can change these settings anytime from your dashboard.

CTASlide._description: Dark gradient call-to-action slide. Use as the LAST slide of every campaign. Drives conversion.

content.headline: (required) Action-oriented headline

primaryButton._note: (optional) Primary CTA. Also accepts cta_primary shape.

secondaryButton._note: (optional) Secondary CTA. Also accepts cta_secondary shape.

content.urgency: (optional) Urgency text in yellow, e.g. 'Only 47 featured spots remaining in your area'
content.backgroundImage: (optional) URL for background image (must start with http)

example.headline: Ready to Get Found?
example.subheadline: It takes less than 5 minutes to claim your listing.
example.urgency: Only 23 featured listing spots remaining in your area

QuoteSlide._description: INTERACTIVE: Displays a professional quote/invoice with line items, totals, and an 'Accept Quote' button. When the user clicks accept, triggers the onAction callback with action='accept_quote'.

content.headline: (optional, also accepts 'title') Section heading. Defaults to 'Your Quote'
content.quote_id: (optional) Backend quote UUID — needed for accept_quote action to work
content.line_items: (required, also accepts 'items') Array of line item objects

line_items.detail: Top of category in your community
Monthly blog post + social media content

CheckoutSlide._description: INTERACTIVE: Stripe payment collection slide. Shows order summary and embedded Stripe Elements payment form. Requires session_id or quote_id to create a PaymentIntent on the backend. Without those IDs, displays a static order summary.

content.headline: (optional, also accepts 'title') Section heading. Defaults to 'Complete Your Purchase'
content.items: (optional, also accepts 'line_items') Array of { description, price } for order summary
content.total: (optional) Override total — if not provided, sums item prices
content.session_id: (optional) Pitch session UUID — triggers backend PaymentIntent creation

example.headline: Complete Your Enrollment
example.success_message: Welcome aboard! Your listing is being set up now.

FormSlide._description: INTERACTIVE: Data capture form with configurable fields. Supports text, email, tel, textarea, select, and number inputs. Validates required fields, optionally submits to a backend endpoint, and fires onAction with action='form_submitted'.

content.headline: (optional, also accepts 'title') Section heading. Defaults to 'Tell Us About Your Business'
content.submit_text: (optional, also accepts 'button_text') Submit button label, default 'Submit'
content.submit_endpoint: (optional) Backend API path to POST form data to
content.success_message: (optional) Message after submit, default 'Thank you! We'll be in touch soon.'

FormSlide._field_types: text, email, tel, textarea, select, number

example.headline: Tell Us About Your Business
example.subheadline: We'll use this to set up your listing.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Brief Description
fields.placeholder: Tell us what makes your business special...

example.success_message: Sarah will start building your listing right away. Check your email for next steps.

slide_order_recommendation: 1. PersonalizedHeroSlide or HeroSlide — Open with impact
3. ConceptSlide (stats) — Back up the problem with data
5. BenefitsSlide or StepSlide — Show value or how it works
narration_tips: Write as Sarah speaking directly to the business owner
Keep each narration 2-4 sentences, conversational tone
Reference what's on screen: 'As you can see here...'
End CTA narrations with encouragement: 'I'm here if you have questions'
Avoid jargon — speak like a helpful advisor, not a salesperson
_GUIDE.audio_file_convention: slide-{NN}-{type}.mp3 where NN is zero-padded slide number and type is lowercase component name without 'Slide' (e.g., slide-01-hero.mp3, slide-03-concept.mp3)
